prompt:
A mirrorless camera on a sturdy tripod is set up in a backyard at night, aimed at a partially eclipsed Moon high in the sky. The camera’s rear screen shows a live view of the Moon transitioning into Earth’s shadow, with subtle reddish tones and no readable UI text. On a nearby table, an open mission logbook displays a sequence of circular sketches of the Moon at different phases of the eclipse, alongside a red flashlight and a remote shutter release resting on the page.

The scene is photorealistic 512×512 near-future solarpunk: silhouettes of houses and rooftop solar arrays frame the lower edge of the image, while the dark sky and faint stars surround the Moon. Lighting is gentle and cinematic, with a soft glow from nearby windows and the red flashlight providing just enough illumination on the logbook without washing out the night. No brand logos or legible labels appear on the camera or accessories, and no full human figures are visible—only equipment and notes—highlighting the process of photographing a lunar eclipse and documenting it.
